issues.description: Test intent/label mismatch: `test_handle_flow_stream_ignores_trailing_error_block_from_initial_content` is named as if trailing error blocks are ignored, but it asserts the trailing error remains persisted (lines 701-704). This reduces diagnostic clarity and conflicts with the default loader behavior that strips trailing error blocks (`backend/services/chat_graph_runner.py:1089-1092`).

codeReview.summary: The feature adds meaningful high-signal backend coverage for run-control cancel-flow behavior, branch/runtime delegation envelopes, and flow persistence ordering/tool-call update semantics, and the worker followed the skill procedure with passing verification evidence. I found one non-blocking clarity issue in a new persistence test where the test name does not match asserted behavior.

summary: Reviewed feature test-reset-rebuild-backend-high-signal-tests via mission metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, worker skill, and commit bf485522ca88c2e39707af1f925ea4b74aa3690b. Overall implementation satisfies VAL-TEST-006 intent with deterministic, behavior-focused backend tests; status is pass with one non-blocking naming/contract-clarity issue.
